title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Anthony McGlass investigates the employment history of the waiter Giuseppe and discovers that the Italian had been present during major thefts at two of his previous hotels. Anthony suspects Giuseppe was sent by an external group rather than acting as a simple thief, noting that Giuseppe's intense reaction to a specific letter suggested a targeted motive. Despite searching through employee inquiries and newspaper advertisements, Anthony is unable to locate him.

Anthony receives a phone call from Mr. Pottsford of Puddington Haggis Publishing, who warns that the manuscript Anthony possesses is being targeted by a dangerous group using bribery and threats. To ensure safety, Pottsford arranges for a company representative, Mr. Holmes, to collect the manuscript from the hotel and provide a £1,000 check. Following Pottsford's advice, Anthony places a fake manuscript in the hotel safe to mislead potential enemies. The following morning, Anthony hands the actual manuscript to Mr. Holmes and receives the payment.

After checking out of the hotel, Anthony receives a formal, cryptic letter regarding the memoirs of the Earl of Stapleton. The document requests that he consult with George Romax and invites him to visit the Chimney House as a guest of the Earl of Chatham on Friday. To protect his identity, Anthony checks into a secluded inn using the alias Anthony Kidd. He writes a letter to the Earl of Carringham, signed "Jimmy McGregor," claiming he has delivered the manuscript to the publishers and is leaving Britain. Meanwhile, Virginia Rue prepares to encounter a blackmailer by shifting her persona from a simple-minded fool to a more alert individual.
